title: Methods systems and apparatus for cultivating densely seeded crops
abstract: A horticultural raft includes a raft body, at least one top cavity, and a group of mid cavities. The top cavity includes an upper face defining a first projected area and a lower face defining a second projected area substantially equal to or less than the first projected area. The upper face provides a seeding pattern having one or two degrees of freedom in a growing medium disposed in the top cavity. Each mid cavity has an upper face defining a third projected area that is entirely contained within the second projected area. The mid cavities are configured to contribute to buoyancy of the floating horticultural raft, allow germinants in the growing medium to communicate via capillary action with a nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution, and mitigate hyperhydration and asphyxiation at respective root-stem junctions of the germinants.
claims:
Claims 1. A horticultural raft (100), comprising: a buoyant raft body (110) having a top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge (108) that defines a top face of the raft; at least a first top cavity (102a) in the buoyant raft body at the top face of the raft and having: a first top cavity upper face that includes at least a portion of the top face of the raft defined by the top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge, the first top cavity upper face having a first projected area; a first top cavity lower face having a second projected area, wherein the second projected area is contained within or equal to the first projected area of the first top cavity upper face; and a first depth (105), from the top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge to the first top cavity lower face, to contain at least one porous horticultural growing medium (120), wherein the first top cavity upper face provides a seeding pattern in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the first top cavity, having two degrees of freedom along the portion of the top face of the raft such that the first top cavity provides a growing bed; and a first plurality of mid cavities (104) in the buoyant raft body and coupled to the first top cavity so as to also contain the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, wherein: the first plurality of mid cavities is arranged as a two-dimensional pattern of individual compartments coupled to the first top cavity; a mid cavity upper face of each mid cavity of the first plurality of mid cavities has a third projected area that is entirely contained within the second projected area of the first top cavity lower face of the first top cavity; a mid cavity lower face of each mid cavity of the first plurality of mid cavities has a fourth projected area that is entirely contained within the third projected area of the mid cavity upper face; and at least a first mid cavity of the first plurality of mid cavities has a second depth (107) from a first upper face of the first mid cavity to a first lower face of the first mid 

 cavity such that the first lower face of the first mid cavity contacts a nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution to allow germinants in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the raft, to communicate via capillary action with the nutrient solution, wherein the second depth (107) of at least the first mid cavity provides a sufficient capillary distance between a safe seeding zone for the germinants in the at least one porous horticultural medium when present in the first top cavity of the raft and the nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution to sufficiently mitigate hyperhydration and asphyxiation at respective root-stem junctions of the germinants. 2. The raft of claim 1, wherein the second depth of at least the first mid cavity avoids substantially submerging the at least one porous horticultural medium when present in the first mid cavity and when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution. 3. The raft of claim 1, wherein the first top cavity upper face is one of a rectangle, an ellipse and a polygon. 4. The raft of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of top cavities (102a, 102b, 102c) including the first top cavity, wherein respective top cavities of the plurality of top cavities provide separate growing beds. 5. The raft of claim 4, wherein each top cavity of the plurality of top cavities is coupled to a corresponding plurality of mid cavities. 6. The raft of claim 1, wherein a top cavity shape of the first top cavity is one of a rectangular prism and a downwards tapered trapezoidal prism. 7. The raft of claim 1, wherein a mid-cavity shape of at least some of the mid cavities is one of a rectangular prism, a cylinder, a downwards tapered trapezoidal prism, and a downward tapered truncated elliptic cone. 8. The raft of claim 1, further comprising the at least one porous horticultural growing medium that continuously and fully fills at least the first top cavity and the plurality of mid cavities, wherein the at least one porous horticultural growing medium includes at least one of: at least one membrane, a foam, a gel, a pourable granular medium and a textile. 9. The raft of claim 8, wherein the at least one porous horticultural growing medium includes the pourable granular medium and at least one of the gel, the foam and the textile. 10. The raft of claim 8, wherein the at least one porous horticultural growing medium includes the pourable granular medium and the at least one membrane. 11. The raft of claim 1, further comprising at least one of a mesh, a screen, or netting to prevent downwards fall-through of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium when present in the raft. 12. The raft of claim 1, wherein the raft body: is constructed from a buoyant solid matrix; includes at least first one air pocket integral to the raft body; and/or is shaped so as to trap air in at least one second air pocket between a lower surface of the raft and the nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution. 13. The raft of claim 1, further comprising: a plurality of bottom cavities (106), wherein each bottom cavity of the plurality of bottom cavities is coupled to a corresponding mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities. 14. The raft of claim 13, further comprising at least one of a mesh, a screen, or netting disposed between each bottom cavity of the plurality of bottom cavities and the corresponding mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities. 15. The raft of claim 13, wherein: 

 each bottom cavity has a bottom cavity upper face having a fifth projected area and a bottom cavity lower face having a sixth projected area; and the sixth projected area of the bottom cavity lower face contains or is contained within the fifth projected area of the bottom cavity upper face. 16. The raft of claim 15, wherein at least some of the bottom cavities of the plurality of bottom cavities have a form of a vertical channel, a collar, or a lip. 17. The raft of claim 16, wherein at least some of the plurality of bottom cavities are sized and arranged with respect to the plurality of mid cavities to provide respective channels to conduct the nutrient solution to the at least one porous horticultural growing medium when present in the plurality of mid cavities, and when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution. 18. The raft of claim 1, wherein the raft body includes at least one air vent. 19. The raft of claim 1, further comprising at least one of a plurality of feet, a plurality of ridges and a plurality of nipples disposed on an underside of the raft body. 20. The raft of claim 1, wherein the first top cavity includes at least one vertical ridge protruding inwards along at least one interior side of the first top cavity to facilitate a downward extension of plant roots of the germinants when present in the raft. 21. The raft of claim 1, wherein at least one of the plurality of mid cavities includes at least one vertical ridge protruding inwards along at least one interior side of the at least one of the plurality of mid cavities to facilitate a downward extension of plant roots of the germinants when present in the raft. 22. The raft of claim 1, further comprising: at least one air vent disposed in the raft body; at least one of a plurality of feet, a plurality of ridges and a plurality of nipples disposed on an underside of the raft body; and 

 at least one vertical ridge protruding inwards along at least one interior side of at least one of the plurality of mid cavities to facilitate a downward extension of plant roots of the germinants when present in the raft. 23. The raft of claim 22, further comprising at least one of a mesh, a screen, or netting to prevent downwards fall-through of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium when present in the raft. 24. The raft of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of interlocking components coupled to the raft body to facilitate reversible interlocking of the raft to at least one other raft, wherein the plurality of interlocking components comprises at least one of physical ties, magnetic ties, rods, stakes, tabs, and grooves. 25. The raft of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of contact points disposed on the raft body to facilitate at least one of robotic and manual handling of the raft. 26. The raft of claim 25, wherein the plurality of contact points comprise a plurality of indentations in the raft body. 27. A horticultural raft (100), comprising: a buoyant raft body (110) having a top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge (108) that defines a top face of the raft; at least a first top cavity (102A) in the buoyant raft body at the top face of the raft and having: a first top cavity upper face that includes at least a portion of the top face of the raft defined by the top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge, the first top cavity upper face having a first projected area; a first top cavity lower face having a second projected area, wherein the second projected area is contained within or equal to the first projected area of the first top cavity upper face; and a first depth (105), from the top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge to the first 

 top cavity lower face, to contain at least one porous horticultural growing medium (120), wherein the first top cavity upper face provides a seeding pattern in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the first top cavity, having two degrees of freedom along the portion of the top face of the raft such that the first top cavity provides a growing bed; and a first plurality of mid cavities (104) in the buoyant raft body and coupled to the first top cavity so as to also contain the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, wherein: the first plurality of mid cavities is arranged as a two-dimensional pattern of individual compartments coupled to the first top cavity; a mid cavity upper face of each mid cavity of the first plurality of mid cavities has a third projected area that is entirely contained within the second projected area of the first top cavity lower face of the first top cavity; a mid cavity lower face of each mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities has a fourth projected area that is entirely contained within the third projected area of the mid cavity upper face; and at least some mid cavities of the first plurality of mid cavities include at least one vertical ridge protruding inwards along at least one interior side of the mid cavity; at least one air vent disposed in the raft body; at least one of a plurality of feet, a plurality of ridges and a plurality of nipples disposed on an underside of the raft body; a plurality of interlocking components coupled to the raft body to facilitate reversible interlocking of the raft to at least one other raft; and a plurality of contact points disposed on the raft body to facilitate at least one of robotic and manual handling of the raft. 28. The raft of claim 27, wherein at least a first mid cavity of the first plurality of mid cavities has a second depth (107) from a first upper face of the first mid cavity to a first lower face of the first mid cavity such that the first lower face of the first mid cavity contacts a nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution to allow germinants in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the raft, to communicate via capillary action with the nutrient solution, wherein the second depth (107) of at least the first mid cavity 

 provides a sufficient capillary distance between a safe seeding zone for the germinants in the at least one porous horticultural medium when present in the first top cavity of the raft and the nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution to sufficiently mitigate hyperhydration and asphyxiation at respective root-stem junctions of the germinants. 29. The raft of claim 29, wherein the at least one porous horticultural growing medium includes the pourable granular medium and at least one of the gel, the foam, and the textile. 31. The raft of claim 29, wherein the at least one porous horticultural growing medium includes the pourable granular medium and the at least one membrane. 32. The raft of claim 27, further comprising at least one of a mesh, a screen, or netting to prevent downwards fall-through of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium when present in the raft. 33. The raft of claim 27, further comprising: a plurality of bottom cavities (106), wherein each bottom cavity of the plurality of bottom cavities is coupled to a corresponding mid cavity of the first plurality of mid cavities. 34. The raft of claim 33, wherein: each bottom cavity has a bottom cavity upper face having a fifth projected area and a bottom cavity lower face having a sixth projected area; and the sixth projected area of the bottom cavity lower face contains or is contained within the fifth projected area of the bottom cavity upper face. 35. The raft of claim 34, wherein at least some of the bottom cavities of the plurality of bottom cavities have a form of a vertical channel, a collar or a lip. 36. The raft of claim 35, wherein at least some of the plurality of bottom cavities are sized and arranged with respect to the plurality of mid cavities to provide respective channels to conduct a nutrient solution to at least one porous horticultural growing medium when present in the plurality of mid cavities, and when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution. 37. A horticultural raft (100), comprising: a buoyant raft body (110) having a top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge (108) that defines a top face of the raft; at least a first top cavity (102A) at the top face of the raft and having: a first top cavity upper face that includes at least a portion of the top face of the raft defined by the top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge, the first top cavity upper face having a first projected area; a first top cavity lower face having a second projected area; and a first depth (105), from the top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge to the first top cavity lower face, to contain at least one porous horticultural growing medium (120), wherein the first top cavity upper face provides a seeding pattern in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the first top cavity, having one degree of freedom or two degrees of freedom along the portion of the top face of the raft; and a plurality of mid cavities (104) coupled to the first top cavity so as to also contain the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, wherein: a mid cavity upper face of each mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities has a third projected area that is entirely contained within the second projected area of the first top cavity lower face of the first top cavity; and the plurality of mid cavities are sized and arranged with respect to the first top cavity to: 1) contribute to buoyancy of the horticultural raft; 2) allow germinants in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the raft, to communicate via capillary action with a nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient 

 solution; and 3) significantly mitigate hyperhydration and asphyxiation at respective root- stem junctions of the germinants. 38. The raft of claim 37, wherein at least a first mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities has a second depth (107) from a first upper face of the first mid cavity to a first lower face of the first mid cavity so as to provide a sufficient capillary distance between: 1) a safe seeding zone for the germinants in the at least one porous horticultural medium when present in the first top cavity of the raft; and 2) the nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution. 41. The raft of claim 52, wherein: the first top cavity upper face has a two-dimensional shape to provide the seeding pattern in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium having the two degrees of freedom along the portion of the top face of the raft. 58. The raft of claim 57, wherein the seeding pattern in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium is homeomorphic to a rectangle. 59. The raft of claim 46, wherein: at least a first mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities has a second depth (107) from a first upper face of the first mid cavity to a first lower face of the first mid cavity; and the plurality of mid cavities are sized and arranged to substantially reduce a quantity of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium as compared to a continuously-filled single-cavity furrow or single-cavity bed of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium having a total depth equal to the first depth (105) plus the second depth (107). 60. The raft of claim 59, wherein the plurality of mid cavities substantially reduce the quantity of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium by at least 40% as compared to the continuously-filled single-cavity furrow or single-cavity bed of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium. 61. The raft of claim 37, wherein the first top cavity upper face provides the seeding pattern in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the first top cavity, having the one degree of freedom. 62. The raft of claim 61, wherein the first top cavity is a furrow (204). 63. The raft of claim 61, further comprising a plurality of top cavities including the first top cavity, wherein respective top cavities of the plurality of top cavities are furrows. 64. The raft of claim 37, wherein the first top cavity upper face provides the seeding pattern in the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the first top cavity, having the two degrees of freedom such that the first top cavity provides a bed. 65. The raft of claim 37, wherein the plurality of mid cavities is arranged as a two- dimensional pattern of individual compartments coupled to the first top cavity. 73. The raft of claim 37, wherein the plurality of mid cavities is arranged as a rectangular grid of individual compartments coupled to the first top cavity. 74. The raft of claim 37, wherein a lower face of each mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities has a fourth projected area that is entirely contained within the third projected area of the mid cavity upper face. 75. The raft of claim 74, wherein a shape of at least some of the mid cavities is one of a rectangular prism, a cylinder, a downwards tapered trapezoidal prism, and a downward tapered truncated elliptic cone. 76. The raft of claim 74, further comprising at least one of a mesh, a screen, or netting disposed at the lower face of each mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities. 77. The raft of claim 37, wherein: at least a first mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities has a second depth (107) from a first upper face of the first mid cavity to a first lower face of the first mid cavity; and the plurality of mid cavities are sized and arranged to substantially reduce a quantity of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the raft, as compared to a continuously-filled single-cavity furrow or bed of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium having a total depth equal to the first depth (105) plus the second depth (107). 78. The raft of claim 77, wherein the plurality of mid cavities substantially reduce the quantity of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium, when present in the raft, by at least 40% as compared to the continuously-filled single-cavity furrow or bed. 79. The raft of claim 37, further comprising: a plurality of bottom cavities (106), wherein each bottom cavity of the plurality of bottom cavities is coupled to a corresponding mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities. 80. The raft of claim 79, further comprising at least one of a mesh, a screen, or netting disposed between each bottom cavity of the plurality of bottom cavities and the corresponding mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities. 81. The raft of claim 79, wherein: each bottom cavity has a bottom cavity upper face having a fifth projected area and a bottom cavity lower face having a sixth projected area; and the sixth projected area of the bottom cavity lower face contains or is contained within the fifth projected area of the bottom cavity upper face. 82. The raft of claim 81, wherein a shape of at least some of the bottom cavities of the plurality of bottom cavities is one of a rectangular prism, a cylinder, a downwards tapered trapezoidal prism, an upwards tapered trapezoidal prism, a downward tapered truncated elliptic cone and an upward tapered truncated elliptic cone. 83. The raft of claim 81, wherein at least some of the bottom cavities of the plurality of bottom cavities have a form of a vertical channel. 84. The raft of claim 81, wherein at least some of the bottom cavities of the plurality of bottom cavities have a form of a collar or a lip. 85.  conduct the nutrient solution to the at least one porous horticultural growing medium when present in the plurality of mid cavities, and when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution. 86. The raft of claim 85, further comprising at least one of a mesh, a screen, or netting disposed between each bottom cavity of the plurality of bottom cavities and the corresponding mid cavity of the plurality of mid cavities. 87. The raft of claim 86, wherein the at least one of the mesh, the screen or netting is constructed and arranged to allow for downwards penetration of plant roots of the germinants while preventing downwards fall-through of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium when present in the plurality of mid cavities. 88. The raft of claim 37, further comprising at least one of a mesh, a screen, or netting to prevent downwards fall-through of the at least one porous horticultural growing medium when present in the raft. 89. The raft of claim 37, wherein at least one of the plurality of mid cavities includes at least one vertical ridge protruding inwards along an interior side of the at least one of the plurality of mid cavities to facilitate a downward extension of plant roots of the germinants when present in the raft. 95. The raft of claim 37, wherein the raft body includes at least one air vent. 96. The raft of claim 37, further comprising at least one buoyant chamber built into the raft body. 97. The raft of claim 37, in combination with a drip tray to hold the raft during non-floating conveyance. 98. The raft of claim 97, wherein the drip tray comprises at least one of internal rails and angled sides to support the raft body on the drip tray while leaving space underneath the raft body and above the drip tray for roots of the germinants when present in the raft. 99. The raft of claim 37, further comprising at least one tag to identify the raft. 100. The raft of claim 99, wherein the at least one tag comprises at least one of a barcode and an RFID chip coupled to the raft body. 101. The raft of claim 37, further comprising at least one sensor coupled to the raft body. 102. The raft of claim 101, wherein the at least one sensor comprises: a wireless data transmitter; and at least one of a battery and a radiant energy converter to provide power to the at least one sensor. 103. The raft of claim 37, further comprising a plurality of contact points disposed on the raft body to facilitate at least one of robotic and manual handling of the raft. 104. The raft of claim 103, wherein the plurality of contact points comprise a plurality of indentations in the raft body. 105. The raft of claim 37, further comprising the at least one porous horticultural growing medium and the germinants, and in combination with a vertically-stacked growing system comprising: a plurality of vertical beams; and a plurality of horizontal shelves mechanically coupled to the plurality of vertical beams and respectively arranged on different vertical levels of the growing system, each horizontal shelf of the plurality of horizontal shelves having a length and a width and comprising: a plurality of horizontal structural supports coupled to at least some of the plurality of vertical beams; decking, coupled to the plurality of horizontal structural supports; a plurality of walls to form a shallow pond when the horizontal shelf contains the nutrient solution, thereby constituting a growing layer of the growing system, the plurality of walls comprising: two side walls along the length of the horizontal shelf; and two end walls along the width of the horizontal shelf, and a loading and/or unloading apparatus to facilitate loading and/or unloading of the raft into and/or out of the shallow pond, via the two end walls, when the horizontal shelf contains the nutrient solution, wherein: at least a first horizontal shelf of the plurality of horizontal shelves further comprises a raft conveyance system to move the raft through a first shallow pond contained in the first horizontal shelf when the first shallow pond and the raft are present in the growing system. 106. The raft of any of claims 37 through 104, further comprising the at least one porous horticultural growing medium and the germinants, and in combination with a vertically-stacked growing system comprising: a plurality of vertical beams; and a plurality of horizontal shelves mechanically coupled to the plurality of vertical beams and respectively arranged on different vertical levels of the growing system, each horizontal shelf of the plurality of horizontal shelves having a length and a width and comprising: a plurality of horizontal structural supports coupled to at least some of the plurality of vertical beams; decking, coupled to the plurality of horizontal structural supports; a plurality of walls to form a shallow pond when the horizontal shelf contains the nutrient solution, thereby constituting a growing layer of the growing system, the plurality of walls comprising: two side walls along the length of the horizontal shelf; and two end walls along the width of the horizontal shelf, and a loading and/or unloading apparatus to facilitate loading and/or unloading of the raft into and/or out of the shallow pond, via the two end walls, when the horizontal shelf contains the nutrient solution, wherein: at least a first horizontal shelf of the plurality of horizontal shelves further comprises a raft conveyance system to move the raft through a first shallow pond contained in the first horizontal shelf when the first shallow pond and the raft are present in the growing system. 107. The raft of claim 37, further comprising the at least one porous horticultural growing medium and the germinants, and in combination with a growing system comprising: at least one horizontal shelf having a length and a width and comprising: decking; a plurality of walls to form a shallow pond when the at least one horizontal shelf contains the nutrient solution, thereby constituting a growing layer of the growing system, the plurality of walls comprising: two side walls along the length of the at least one horizontal shelf; and 

 two end walls along the width of the at least one horizontal shelf, and a loading and/or unloading apparatus to facilitate loading and/or unloading of the raft into and/or out of the shallow pond, via the two end walls, when the at least one horizontal shelf contains the nutrient solution. 108. The raft of claim 107, wherein: the at least one horizontal shelf further comprises a raft conveyance system to move the raft through the shallow pond when the shallow pond and the raft are present in the growing system. 109. The raft of claims 37 through 104, further comprising the at least one porous horticultural growing medium and the germinants, and in combination with a growing system comprising: at least one horizontal shelf having a length and a width and comprising: decking; a plurality of walls to form a shallow pond when the at least one horizontal shelf contains the nutrient solution, thereby constituting a growing layer of the growing system, the plurality of walls comprising: two side walls along the length of the at least one horizontal shelf; and two end walls along the width of the at least one horizontal shelf, and a loading and/or unloading apparatus to facilitate loading and/or unloading of the raft into and/or out of the shallow pond, via the two end walls, when the at least one horizontal shelf contains the nutrient solution. 110. A horticultural raft (100), comprising: a raft body (110) having a top-facing non-seedbearing perimeter edge (108) that defines a top face of the raft; first means for containing at least one horticultural growing medium, wherein the first means provides a seeding pattern in the at least one horticultural growing medium, when present in the raft, having one degree of freedom or two degrees of freedom along the portion of the top face of the raft; and second means for containing the at least one horticultural growing medium, wherein the 

 second means: 1) allows germinants in the at least one horticultural growing medium, when present in the raft, to communicate via capillary action with a nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution; and 2) significantly mitigates hyperhydration and asphyxiation at respective root-stem junctions of the germinants. 111. The raft of claim 110, wherein: the first means has a first depth (105); the second means has a second depth (107); and the second means substantially reduce a quantity of the at least one horticultural growing medium as compared to a continuously-filled single-cavity furrow or single-cavity bed of the at least one horticultural growing medium having a total depth equal to the first depth (105) plus the second depth (107). 112. The raft of claim 110, wherein the second means provides a sufficient capillary distance between a safe seeding zone for the germinants in the at least one horticultural medium when present in the raft and the nutrient solution when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution. 113. The raft of claim 112, wherein the second means avoids substantially submerging the at least one horticultural medium when present in the raft and when the raft is floating in the nutrient solution. 114. The raft of claim 113, wherein: the first means has a first depth (105); the second means has a second depth (107); and the second means substantially reduce a quantity of the at least one horticultural growing medium as compared to a continuously-filled single-cavity furrow or single-cavity bed of the at least one horticultural growing medium having a total depth equal to the first depth (105) plus the second depth (107). 115. The raft of claim 110, further comprising means for buoyancy to facilitate floating of the raft in the nutrient solution. 116. The raft of any of claims 110 through 114, further comprising means for buoyancy to facilitate floating of the raft in the nutrient solution. 117.
